business_name: Bellymack Hill Farm Red Kite Feeding Station
location: Bellymack Hill Farm, Laurieston, Castle Douglas, DG7 2PJ
why_them: No functional website — domain returns NXDOMAIN. Runs entirely off Facebook and third-party listings (TripAdvisor, Galloway Kite Trail). Charges £7.50 adult admission with daily feeding sessions drawing over 100 kites, so there is real commercial volume with zero owned digital presence.
recommended_service: New website with admission pricing, seasonal opening hours, daily feeding schedule, photo gallery, Google Maps integration and Stripe/PayPal ticket widget
source: Google search + Galloway Kite Trail listing + TripAdvisor

why_them: Existing WordPress site has good bones but bounces users to Eventbrite for ticketing, fragmenting the experience. Email address is obfuscated via JavaScript, making it invisible to many visitors. No integrated shop, no FAQ for groups or school visits, and seasonal closure is buried rather than prominently communicated.
recommended_service: Website refresh with integrated Stripe ticketing, embedded events calendar, grouped visit enquiry form, and a basic online shop for books/prints

location: High Street, Gatehouse of Fleet, Castle Douglas, DG7 2HP
why_them: Divi-built WordPress site with no visible ticketing, no online booking for events/exhibitions, and no dynamic events calendar — everything is static text. As an 18th-century cotton mill with a cafe, craft shop, and changing exhibition programme, it needs a site that actively sells visits, not just describes them.
recommended_service: New events calendar, integrated Stripe ticketing for exhibitions, cafe table booking widget, and SEO content targeting 'things to do Gatehouse of Fleet'

location: 45-47 George Street, Whithorn, Newton Stewart, DG8 8NS
why_them: Has some digital infrastructure but tickets click through to an external page rather than inline checkout, creating friction. Opening hours are static text with no live availability, there is no group booking workflow, and the site is slow on mobile due to heavy JavaScript and emoji-loading scripts.
recommended_service: Inline Stripe ticketing integration, group booking enquiry form, performance optimisation, and an improved mobile-first redesign to reduce bounce rate

business_name: Mull of Galloway Lighthouse Visitor Centre
location: Mull of Galloway, Drummore, Stranraer, DG9 9HP
why_them: Divi-built site with no pricing, no online tickets or donation widget, no interactive event calendar, and no way to check lighthouse opening in advance. Donation-only admission means it is voluntarily leaving revenue on the table; a well-designed site with a visible donate/pre-visit flow could materially increase per-visitor yield.
recommended_service: New visitor information site with prominent donation/ticketing CTA, foghorn demonstration schedule, interactive opening calendar, and lighthouse climb booking

why_them: Has a functional Elementor site with booking buttons, but pricing is completely hidden. Reviewers specifically note the online booking system is 'flaky' and recommend calling instead. Five rooms in a 900-year-old castle is a compelling story that the current site under-sells photographically.
recommended_service: Redesign with transparent per-person pricing table, reliable integrated booking system (e.g. Bookwhen or FareHarbor), improved castle photography, and gift voucher purchase flow
source: Google search + WebFetch assessment + Great British Life

why_them: D&G's only dedicated indoor tactical laser tag arena operates entirely on Facebook with no website. They ran a Crowdfunder in 2017 to establish the arena, confirming it is a real, trading business. Without a website they cannot take advance bookings, sell party packages, or appear in Google search — all revenue is walk-in or Facebook-referral.
recommended_service: New website with arena description, pricing, online booking for sessions and birthday parties, waiver/consent form integration, and Google Business Profile setup
source: Google search + Facebook page + Crowdfunder listing

why_them: Wix site with professional structure but zero pricing transparency and no bookable online calendar — all bookings go via phone or email to a personal mobile number. An 11,000-acre estate with over 30 shooting stands, rifle range, and corporate experience packages needs a site that can quote, capture enquiries and take deposits automatically.
recommended_service: Wix-to-custom rebuild with transparent package pricing, online enquiry/deposit form, corporate events landing page, and an experience photography gallery

location: St Mary's Isle Estate, Kirkcudbright, DG6 4JZ
why_them: Squarespace site looks clean but lists zero pricing, has no availability calendar or booking system, and relies entirely on phone/email contact. A family-owned 2,000-acre estate offering driven pheasant and partridge shooting with sea views across the Solway is exactly the kind of premium product that sells itself online if the site is built to convert.
recommended_service: New site with shoot package descriptions, per-gun pricing, season dates calendar, deposit payment via Stripe, and a photo/video gallery of the estate

why_them: Astra WordPress theme with a heavy Master Slider plugin, no visible pricing, and no booking mechanism. Offers deer stalking (red and roe), wild fowling, rough shooting, and accommodation — a broad product range that needs structured landing pages per activity to capture search traffic and convert enquiries without requiring a phone call.
recommended_service: Activity-specific landing pages with pricing, an online enquiry and deposit system, and SEO targeting 'deer stalking Scotland' and 'wild fowling Galloway'

location: Rusko, Gatehouse of Fleet, Castle Douglas, DG7
why_them: Elementor WordPress site with good photography but no integrated payment — all course bookings require phoning or emailing Liz directly on a personal mobile and email. Residential 5-day art holiday packages at £300pp plus weekly classes at £25/session represent real recurring revenue, all of which is funnelled through manual phone/email. 'SOLD OUT' tags show demand exists.
recommended_service: New site with inline Stripe payment for course deposits, availability calendar, gift voucher purchase, and a dedicated landing page optimised for 'art holidays Scotland'

location: Loch Ken Marina, Parton, Castle Douglas, DG7 3NF
why_them: The domain skilochken.co.uk shows only a 'coming soon' placeholder — effectively no website. The business is actively trading with positive TripAdvisor reviews for waterskiing, wakeboarding, banana boats and ringos on Loch Ken. All bookings and enquiries are through Facebook and a hotmail email address, meaning the business is invisible to search.
recommended_service: New website with activity descriptions, session pricing, online booking calendar, birthday/group party packages, waiver form, and Google Business Profile
source: Google search + TripAdvisor + BWSW club listing + WebFetch

why_them: Luxury hot tub hire operator across Dumfries & Galloway with Instagram and Facebook as its only online presence — no website. Offering includes fully disinfected, insured tubs available 24/7. Without a website they cannot rank for 'hot tub hire Dumfries', show availability, or take bookings outside of social DMs.
recommended_service: Simple 5-page website with hire packages and pricing, availability request form, FAQs on hygiene/setup, service area map, and Google Business Profile setup

business_name: Isle Adventures Ltd (Lauren Jade Wildlife & Fishing Trips)
location: Isle of Whithorn Harbour, Newton Stewart, DG8
why_them: Operates chartered wildlife and sea angling boat trips on the Lauren Jade from Isle of Whithorn with no website whatsoever — found only via TripAdvisor reviews and a LinkedIn profile. Captain Derek Farr has 30+ years of naturalist and skipper experience and receives excellent reviews, but the business is completely unsearchable and relies entirely on word-of-mouth and repeat customers.
recommended_service: New website with trip types (wildlife watching vs. sea angling), pricing, half/full-day availability, booking request form, gallery of species seen, and TripAdvisor review integration
source: Google search + TripAdvisor + VisitScotland wildlife cruise listing

why_them: WordPress site with online booking forms present but homepage pricing is hidden, body text is truncated mid-sentence, and SEO is weak due to heavy JavaScript reliance. At £11 admission and 5,000sqft of indoor play, this is a well-established seasonal attraction that could convert better with a content and pricing refresh.
recommended_service: Homepage pricing overhaul, content audit and rewrite, SEO improvements targeting 'farm park Dumfries' and 'family day out Dumfries', and season pass upsell flow

location: Barstobrick Estate, Ringford, Castle Douglas, DG7 2AB
why_them: Website loads as a JavaScript redirect to '/lander' with no actual content served — visitors see a blank or perpetual loading screen. The underlying domain is live but non-functional as a digital presence. All bookings go via a personal mobile and email. A BHS-qualified centre with indoor arena, outdoor school, trekking over 650 acres, and a coffee shop needs a working site to capture the D&G visitor market.
recommended_service: Full new website replacing the broken redirect, with trek and lesson pricing, online booking, meet-the-ponies photo gallery, and Google Maps directions
